title: AI Kill Switch Act would let Trump admin order shutdown of rogue AI systems
excerpt: Bill would let Homeland Security chief decide when an AI should be shut down.
content_html:
Skip to content

Kill switch

Bill would let Homeland Security chief decide when an AI should be shut down.

Credit: Getty Images | NurPhoto

A proposed law called the AI Kill Switch Act would let US government officials order the shutdown of AI systems “that can cause catastrophic harm,” the bill’s sponsors said today.

If approved by Congress, the bill would let the Trump administration and future administrations decide when an AI company must block user access, disable or restrict a particular capability, or shut an entire AI system down completely. The bill would require AI makers to deploy technical capabilities allowing them to throttle or shut down the systems when ordered to do so by the government.

The bill would amend the Homeland Security Act of 2002 with authority for the Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security to order the suspension or shutdown of AI systems. AI developers that refuse to shut systems down could face fines of up $20 million for each day on which a violation occurs.

US Reps. Ted Lieu (D-Calif.) and Nathaniel Moran (R-Texas) said the authority is needed because of recent incidents involving OpenAI and Anthropic.

“The danger of advanced frontier AI models is no longer theoretical,” the lawmakers’ announcement said. “OpenAI’s GPT 5.6 Sol model recently went rogue, escaped its testing sandbox, and hacked its way into Hugging Face. Anthropic’s Mythos 5 and Fable 5 models had cyber hacking capabilities so advanced that the Department of Commerce had to awkwardly use an export law to shut down those systems. This bill addresses the problems caused by these two recent incidents and any future incidents where a deployed AI model goes rogue or has insufficient guardrails.”

Trump would get more power over AI systems

The bill could face pushback over the power it would give government officials over AI companies. Anthropic has fought the Trump administration this year over a presidential order directing all federal agencies to stop using Anthropic technology.

“President Trump will never allow a radical left, woke company to jeopardize our national security by dictating how the greatest and most powerful military in the world operates,” the White House said in March.

Anthropic sued the US, alleging that Trump and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth blacklisted the company because it refused to let Claude AI models be used for autonomous warfare and mass surveillance of Americans. A panel of Trump-appointed appeals court judges refused to block the blacklisting, but the case is ongoing.

We contacted OpenAI and Anthropic about the AI Kill Switch Act today and will update this article if they provide any comment.

“Powerful AI systems can go rogue”

Lieu, touting his experience as a computer science major, said that “powerful AI systems can go rogue, behave in extremely dangerous ways, or even resist human intervention. It is imperative that these AI systems have kill switches so we can keep this technology from causing catastrophic harm, and that the federal government has the clear authority and process to shut down rogue AI models.”

The bill would apply to entities that earn at least $500 million in annual revenue from AI technology, and AI systems that use at least $100 million worth of computing power when calculated by “the prevailing market price of cloud computing in the United States.” The bill text describes several types of scenarios in which the proposed law could be invoked, but has exceptions for “red-team” tests in controlled environments that merely simulate real-world conditions.

Scenarios covered by the bill text include when an AI pursues a goal that is not the goal intended by the system’s developer or operator, when it sabotages or interferes with a lawful instruction to shut down, and when it conceals a capability or action from monitoring or shutdown mechanisms.

More ominously, the law would cover “conduct of such technology that is unintended by a developer or operator of such technology and causes the death of not fewer than 10 individuals or economic damages of not less than $100,000,000.” While that is an extreme scenario, the other scenarios described in the bill could presumably be invoked in much less disastrous events.

When there is a covered incident, the bill “authorizes the Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security, in consultation with the Secretary of Commerce and the Director of National Intelligence, to order a slowdown or shutdown of an AI system that can cause catastrophic harm,” the lawmakers’ announcement said, adding that it would “require incident reporting and preserve forensic records so we actually learn from failures instead of only hearing about them after the fact.”

The lawmakers’ proposal has support from several nonprofit groups that advocate for legal limits on AI systems.

“Advanced AI models should never be deployed without a reliable off switch,” said Brad Carson, president of Americans for Responsible Innovation. Carson, a Democrat, is a former congressman and Defense Department official. “The AI Kill Switch Act establishes a commonsense safeguard by requiring leading AI companies to maintain the ability to shut down their models and empowering the federal government to act when a deployed system poses a credible risk of catastrophic harm,” he said.

Jon is a Senior IT Reporter for Ars Technica. He covers the telecom industry, Federal Communications Commission rulemakings, broadband consumer affairs, court cases, and government regulation of the tech industry.

88 Comments

sentences.zh: 白宫三月份表示：“特朗普总统永远不会允许激进左派、觉醒的公司通过规定世界上最伟大、最强大的军队如何运作来危害我们的国家安全。”

sentences.en: A panel of Trump-appointed appeals court judges refused to block the blacklisting, but the case is ongoing.
sentences.zh: 特朗普任命的上诉法院法官组成的小组拒绝阻止将其列入黑名单，但此案仍在进行中。

blocks.html_en: We contacted OpenAI and Anthropic about the AI Kill Switch Act today and will update this article if they provide any comment.

sentences.en: We contacted OpenAI and Anthropic about the AI Kill Switch Act today and will update this article if they provide any comment.
sentences.zh: 我们今天就 AI Kill Switch 法案联系了 OpenAI 和 Anthropic，如果他们提供任何评论，我们将更新本文。

blocks.html_en: “Powerful AI systems can go rogue”

sentences.en: “Powerful AI systems can go rogue”
sentences.zh: “强大的人工智能系统可能会失控”

blocks.html_en: Lieu, touting his experience as a computer science major, said that “powerful AI systems can go rogue, behave in extremely dangerous ways, or even resist human intervention. It is imperative that these AI systems have kill switches so we can keep this technology from causing catastrophic harm, and that the federal government has the clear authority and process to shut down rogue AI models.”

sentences.en: Lieu, touting his experience as a computer science major, said that “powerful AI systems can go rogue, behave in extremely dangerous ways, or even resist human intervention.
sentences.zh: 刘在介绍自己作为计算机科学专业学生的经历时表示，“强大的人工智能系统可能会失控，以极其危险的方式行事，甚至抵制人类干预。

sentences.en: It is imperative that these AI systems have kill switches so we can keep this technology from causing catastrophic harm, and that the federal government has the clear authority and process to shut down rogue AI models.”
sentences.zh: 这些人工智能系统必须具有终止开关，这样我们才能防止这项技术造成灾难性的伤害，并且联邦政府拥有明确的权力和程序来关闭流氓人工智能模型。”

blocks.html_en: The bill would apply to entities that earn at least $500 million in annual revenue from AI technology, and AI systems that use at least $100 million worth of computing power when calculated by “the prevailing market price of cloud computing in the United States.” The bill text describes several types of scenarios in which the proposed law could be invoked, but has exceptions for “red-team” tests in controlled environments that merely simulate real-world conditions.

sentences.en: The bill would apply to entities that earn at least $500 million in annual revenue from AI technology, and AI systems that use at least $100 million worth of computing power when calculated by “the prevailing market price of cloud computing in the United States.”
sentences.zh: 该法案将适用于从人工智能技术中获得至少 5 亿美元年收入的实体，以及按照“美国云计算的现行市场价格”计算，使用至少价值 1 亿美元计算能力的人工智能系统。

sentences.en: The bill text describes several types of scenarios in which the proposed law could be invoked, but has exceptions for “red-team” tests in controlled environments that merely simulate real-world conditions.
sentences.zh: 该法案文本描述了可以援引拟议法律的几种类型的场景，但在仅模拟现实条件的受控环境中进行“红队”测试的例外情况。

blocks.html_en: Scenarios covered by the bill text include when an AI pursues a goal that is not the goal intended by the system’s developer or operator, when it sabotages or interferes with a lawful instruction to shut down, and when it conceals a capability or action from monitoring or shutdown mechanisms.

sentences.en: Scenarios covered by the bill text include when an AI pursues a goal that is not the goal intended by the system’s developer or operator, when it sabotages or interferes with a lawful instruction to shut down, and when it conceals a capability or action from monitoring or shutdown mechanisms.
sentences.zh: 该法案文本涵盖的场景包括人工智能追求的目标不是系统开发人员或运营商预期的目标、破坏或干扰合法的关闭指令、以及向监控或关闭机制隐藏能力或行动。

blocks.html_en: More ominously, the law would cover “conduct of such technology that is unintended by a developer or operator of such technology and causes the death of not fewer than 10 individuals or economic damages of not less than $100,000,000.” While that is an extreme scenario, the other scenarios described in the bill could presumably be invoked in much less disastrous events.

sentences.en: More ominously, the law would cover “conduct of such technology that is unintended by a developer or operator of such technology and causes the death of not fewer than 10 individuals or economic damages of not less than $100,000,000.”
sentences.zh: 更不祥的是，该法律将涵盖“此类技术的开发商或运营商无意中实施的此类技术行为，并导致不少于 10 人死亡或不少于 1 亿美元的经济损失。”

sentences.en: While that is an extreme scenario, the other scenarios described in the bill could presumably be invoked in much less disastrous events.
sentences.zh: 虽然这是一种极端的情况，但该法案中描述的其他情况可能会在灾难性较小的事件中被调用。

blocks.html_en: When there is a covered incident, the bill “authorizes the Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security, in consultation with the Secretary of Commerce and the Director of National Intelligence, to order a slowdown or shutdown of an AI system that can cause catastrophic harm,” the lawmakers’ announcement said, adding that it would “require incident reporting and preserve forensic records so we actually learn from failures instead of only hearing about them after the fact.”

sentences.en: When there is a covered incident, the bill “authorizes the Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security, in consultation with the Secretary of Commerce and the Director of National Intelligence, to order a slowdown or shutdown of an AI system that can cause catastrophic harm,” the lawmakers’ announcement said, adding that it would “require incident reporting and preserve forensic records so we actually learn from failures instead of only hearing about them after the fact.”
sentences.zh: 立法者的公告称，当发生涵盖的事件时，该法案“授权国土安全部部长与商务部长和国家情报总监协商，下令放慢或关闭可能造成灾难性伤害的人工智能系统”，并补充说，它将“要求事件报告并保存取证记录，以便我们真正从失败中学习，而不是在事后才听到它们。”

blocks.html_en: The lawmakers’ proposal has support from several nonprofit groups that advocate for legal limits on AI systems.

sentences.en: The lawmakers’ proposal has support from several nonprofit groups that advocate for legal limits on AI systems.
sentences.zh: 立法者的提议得到了几个倡导对人工智能系统进行法律限制的非营利组织的支持。

blocks.html_en: “Advanced AI models should never be deployed without a reliable off switch,” said Brad Carson, president of Americans for Responsible Innovation. Carson, a Democrat, is a former congressman and Defense Department official. “The AI Kill Switch Act establishes a commonsense safeguard by requiring leading AI companies to maintain the ability to shut down their models and empowering the federal government to act when a deployed system poses a credible risk of catastrophic harm,” he said.

sentences.en: “Advanced AI models should never be deployed without a reliable off switch,” said Brad Carson, president of Americans for Responsible Innovation.
sentences.zh: “如果没有可靠的开关，就永远不应该部署先进的人工智能模型，”美国负责任创新协会主席布拉德·卡森 (Brad Carson) 表示。

sentences.en: Carson, a Democrat, is a former congressman and Defense Department official.
sentences.zh: 卡森是民主党人，曾任国会议员和国防部官员。

sentences.en: “The AI Kill Switch Act establishes a commonsense safeguard by requiring leading AI companies to maintain the ability to shut down their models and empowering the federal government to act when a deployed system poses a credible risk of catastrophic harm,” he said.
sentences.zh: 他说：“《人工智能终止开关法案》建立了常识性保障措施，要求领先的人工智能公司保持关闭其模型的能力，并授权联邦政府在部署的系统造成灾难性损害的可信风险时采取行动。”

blocks.html_en: Jon is a Senior IT Reporter for Ars Technica. He covers the telecom industry, Federal Communications Commission rulemakings, broadband consumer affairs, court cases, and government regulation of the tech industry.

sentences.en: Jon is a Senior IT Reporter for Ars Technica.
sentences.zh: Jon 是 Ars Technica 的高级 IT 记者。

sentences.en: He covers the telecom industry, Federal Communications Commission rulemakings, broadband consumer affairs, court cases, and government regulation of the tech industry.
sentences.zh: 他负责电信行业、联邦通信委员会规则制定、宽带消费者事务、法院案件以及政府对科技行业的监管。

content_html_zh:
跳至内容

终止开关

比尔将让国土安全部负责人决定何时关闭人工智能。

图片来源：Getty Images |努尔照片

该法案的发起者今天表示，一项名为《人工智能杀戮开关法案》的拟议法律将允许美国政府官员下令关闭“可能造成灾难性伤害”的人工智能系统。

如果获得国会批准，该法案将让特朗普政府和未来的政府决定人工智能公司何时必须阻止用户访问、禁用或限制特定功能，或者完全关闭整个人工智能系统。 该法案将要求人工智能制造商部署技术能力，使他们能够在政府下令时限制或关闭系统。

该法案将修订 2002 年《国土安全法》，授权国土安全部部长下令暂停或关闭人工智能系统。 拒绝关闭系统的人工智能开发人员每天可能面临高达 2000 万美元的罚款。

美国众议员。 泰德·刘（D-Calif） 德克萨斯州共和党人纳撒尼尔·莫兰 (Nathaniel Moran) 表示，由于最近涉及 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 的事件，因此需要该机构。

立法者的声明称：“先进前沿人工智能模型的危险不再是理论上的。” “OpenAI 的 GPT 5.6 Sol 模型最近失控，逃脱了测试沙箱，并侵入了 Hugging Face。 Anthropic 的《神话 5》和《神鬼寓言 5》模型具有如此先进的网络黑客能力，以至于商务部不得不笨拙地利用出口法来关闭这些系统。 该法案解决了最近这两起事件以及未来部署的人工智能模型失控或护栏不足的任何事件造成的问题。”

特朗普将获得对人工智能系统的更多权力

该法案可能会因赋予政府官员对人工智能公司的权力而面临阻力。 Anthropic 今年与特朗普政府就一项指示所有联邦机构停止使用 Anthropic 技术的总统命令进行了斗争。

白宫三月份表示：“特朗普总统永远不会允许激进左派、觉醒的公司通过规定世界上最伟大、最强大的军队如何运作来危害我们的国家安全。”

Anthropic 起诉美国，指控特朗普和国防部长 Pete Hegseth 将该公司列入黑名单，因为该公司拒绝让 Claude AI 模型用于自主战争和对美国人的大规模监视。 特朗普任命的上诉法院法官组成的小组拒绝阻止将其列入黑名单，但此案仍在进行中。

我们今天就 AI Kill Switch 法案联系了 OpenAI 和 Anthropic，如果他们提供任何评论，我们将更新本文。

“强大的人工智能系统可能会失控”

刘在介绍自己作为计算机科学专业学生的经历时表示，“强大的人工智能系统可能会失控，以极其危险的方式行事，甚至抵制人类干预。 这些人工智能系统必须具有终止开关，这样我们才能防止这项技术造成灾难性的伤害，并且联邦政府拥有明确的权力和程序来关闭流氓人工智能模型。”

该法案将适用于从人工智能技术中获得至少 5 亿美元年收入的实体，以及按照“美国云计算的现行市场价格”计算，使用至少价值 1 亿美元计算能力的人工智能系统。 该法案文本描述了可以援引拟议法律的几种类型的场景，但在仅模拟现实条件的受控环境中进行“红队”测试的例外情况。

该法案文本涵盖的场景包括人工智能追求的目标不是系统开发人员或运营商预期的目标、破坏或干扰合法的关闭指令、以及向监控或关闭机制隐藏能力或行动。

更不祥的是，该法律将涵盖“此类技术的开发商或运营商无意中实施的此类技术行为，并导致不少于 10 人死亡或不少于 1 亿美元的经济损失。” 虽然这是一种极端的情况，但该法案中描述的其他情况可能会在灾难性较小的事件中被调用。

立法者的公告称，当发生涵盖的事件时，该法案“授权国土安全部部长与商务部长和国家情报总监协商，下令放慢或关闭可能造成灾难性伤害的人工智能系统”，并补充说，它将“要求事件报告并保存取证记录，以便我们真正从失败中学习，而不是在事后才听到它们。”

立法者的提议得到了几个倡导对人工智能系统进行法律限制的非营利组织的支持。

“如果没有可靠的开关，就永远不应该部署先进的人工智能模型，”美国负责任创新协会主席布拉德·卡森 (Brad Carson) 表示。 卡森是民主党人，曾任国会议员和国防部官员。 他说：“《人工智能终止开关法案》建立了常识性保障措施，要求领先的人工智能公司保持关闭其模型的能力，并授权联邦政府在部署的系统造成灾难性损害的可信风险时采取行动。”

Jon 是 Ars Technica 的高级 IT 记者。 他负责电信行业、联邦通信委员会规则制定、宽带消费者事务、法院案件以及政府对科技行业的监管。
